sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: HC rejects P&G’s exclusive claim over the term ‘vapo’
feed.summary: The Madras High Court rejected Procter & Gamble's claim of exclusive rights over the term 'vapo,' stating it is merely an abbreviation of 'vapour' and is descriptive and common to the trade. Justice N Senthilkumar observed that 'vapo' is publici juris, meaning it belongs to the public, and therefore, P&G cannot claim an exclusive right to its use. The court also found that the trademarks 'Vicks VapoRub' and IPI India's 'Vaporin Cold Rub' and 'Vaporin' are distinct and not deceptively similar, both visually and phonetically.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (URI) United Rentals, Inc. Reports Q4 Revenue $4.21B, vs. FactSet Est of $4.24B
feed.summary: United Rentals, Inc. (URI) reported its fourth-quarter revenue of $4.21 billion, falling slightly short of FactSet's estimate of $4.24 billion. Concurrently, the company also announced Q4 adjusted EPS of $11.09 per share, which was below FactSet's estimated $11.79 per share. These financial results were released on January 28, 2026.

feed.title: Kid Rock to Congress: For Fans and Artists, the Live Nation-Ticketmaster “Experiment Has Failed Miserably”
feed.summary: Kid Rock testified before Congress, urging lawmakers to address the exorbitant costs and deceptive practices in the live event ticketing industry, primarily concerning Live Nation-Ticketmaster. He highlighted how the merger has negatively impacted artists and fans, and proposed solutions like capping resale prices and enforcing the BOTS Act. This testimony comes amidst ongoing antitrust lawsuits against Live Nation Entertainment.

feed.title: NiSource to Release Full-Year 2025 Financial Results and Host Conference Call on February 11
feed.summary: NiSource Inc. announced it will release its full-year 2025 financial results on February 11, 2026, before U.S. financial markets open. The company will also host a conference call on the same day at 11 a.m. EST to discuss these results and provide a business update. A replay of the call will be available online and via phone from February 11 to February 18, 2026.

feed.title: Corteva, Inc. (NYSE:CTVA) Announces $0.18 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Corteva, Inc. (NYSE:CTVA) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, which translates to an annualized dividend of $0.72 and a yield of approximately 1.0%. The company has consistently increased its dividend for the past five years, with a sustainable payout ratio of about 20.5%. Corteva recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates for both EPS and revenue, and its stock is trading near $73.

feed.title: ServiceNow and Fiserv expand strategic commitment to accelerate AI-driven transformation of financial services
feed.summary: ServiceNow and Fiserv have announced an expanded strategic commitment to accelerate AI-driven transformation within financial services. Fiserv will scale its use of ServiceNow's Now Assist for Financial Services Operations (FSO) and IT Service Management (ITSM) to improve operational excellence, resiliency, and stability across its IT and client experiences. This collaboration aims to leverage AI to manage increasing transaction volumes and regulatory complexity, enabling more proactive and efficient operations.

feed.title: ServiceNow Inc earnings beat by $0.03, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: ServiceNow Inc (NYSE: NOW) reported strong fourth-quarter results, beating analyst estimates for both earnings per share and revenue. The company posted an EPS of $0.92, surpassing the $0.89 estimate, and achieved revenue of $3.57 billion against a consensus of $3.53 billion. Despite the positive earnings, the company's stock has seen a significant decline over the past 3 and 12 months, though its financial health is currently rated as "good performance."

feed.title: KKR finance chief’s Bank of America talk will stream live Feb. 10
feed.summary: KKR & Co. Inc. announced that its Chief Financial Officer, Robert H. Lewin, will present at the Bank of America Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference on February 10, 2026. A live webcast of the presentation will be available on KKR's Investor Center website, with a replay accessible shortly after the event. KKR is a global investment firm specializing in alternative asset management, capital markets, and insurance solutions.

feed.title: United Rentals Announces Fourth Quarter and Full-Year¹ 2025 Results, Introduces 2026 Outlook for Growth, and Announces Plan to Return Approximately $2 Billion to Shareholders in 2026 Supported by New $5 Billion Share Repurchase Program
feed.summary: United Rentals (NYSE: URI) announced strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year 2025, with record revenue and adjusted EBITDA. The company also introduced its 2026 outlook, projecting another year of profitable growth and strong free cash flow. In a significant move to enhance shareholder returns, United Rentals plans to repurchase $1.5 billion of common stock in 2026, supported by a new $5 billion share repurchase program, and has increased its quarterly dividend by 10%.

feed.title: ServiceNow inks another AI partnership, this time with Anthropic
feed.summary: ServiceNow has announced a multi-year partnership with AI research lab Anthropic, just a week after a similar deal with OpenAI. This new collaboration integrates Anthropic's Claude model family into ServiceNow's platform for customers and its own employees, making Claude the preferred AI model for its workflow products and AI agent builder. ServiceNow emphasizes a multi-model strategy to offer enterprise customers choice, with each AI model providing different strengths to be orchestrated on the ServiceNow AI Platform.

feed.title: Webster Financial sets Feb. 19 payout for $0.40-a-share dividend
feed.summary: Webster Financial Corporation announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.40 per share on its common stock, payable on February 19, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 9, 2026. The company also declared dividends for its Series F and Series G Preferred Stocks, with varying payout dates in March and April 2026. Webster Financial, headquartered in Stamford, CT, is a commercial bank with over $80 billion in assets, offering financial services across Commercial Banking, Healthcare Financial Services, and Consumer Banking.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (CHRW) C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. Reports Q4 Revenue $3.91B, vs. FactSet Est of $3.99B
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. (CHRW) announced its Q4 revenue of $3.91 billion, which fell short of FactSet's estimate of $3.99 billion. This report comes alongside news of the company's Q4 adjusted earnings rising, despite a decline in revenue.

feed.title: Blackstone mulls deeper bet on Oracle's Michigan data center - report (BX:NYSE)
feed.summary: Blackstone (BX) is reportedly considering a larger financial involvement in Oracle's (ORCL) Michigan data center, potentially including a debt investment. Bank of America (BAC) is currently leading efforts to raise debt for the project. This move suggests Blackstone's continued interest in infrastructure and data center investments.

feed.title: U.S. Markets Ended Wednesday Mixed As Seagate Technology Led, Carvana Lagged
feed.summary: U.S. stock indexes closed mixed on Wednesday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average gaining 0.17%. The S&P 500 saw a slight increase of 0.02%, while the Nasdaq remained flat. Seagate Technology notably led the market, and Carvana lagged behind other stocks.

feed.title: Kenvue Inc. to Issue Dividend of $0.21 (NYSE:KVUE)
feed.summary: Kenvue Inc. (NYSE:KVUE) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.2075 per share, payable on February 25th to shareholders of record by February 11th. The dividend is supported by a payout ratio of 68.0%, with analysts projecting future earnings per share to adequately cover the annualized dividend. The company's stock traded at $17.36 midday on Wednesday, with a market capitalization of $33.25 billion.

feed.title: American Intelligence & Power Forms Strategic Alliance with Caterpillar and Boyd CAT to Deploy 2 Gigawatts of Dedicated Power for Hyperscale AI Infrastructure
feed.summary: American Intelligence & Power Corporation (AIP Corp), Caterpillar Inc., and Boyd CAT have formed a strategic alliance and executed a purchase agreement to develop AIP Corp's Monarch Compute Campus. This initiative aims to deploy 2 gigawatts (GW) of dedicated power by 2027 for hyperscale AI infrastructure, leveraging fast-response natural gas generator sets and battery energy storage systems. The project is designed to provide reliable and scalable power without increasing costs for existing utility customers, with plans for future expansion up to 8 GW.

feed.title: Southern Co (SO) Maintains Quarterly Dividend at $0.74 Per Share
feed.summary: Southern Co (SO) has announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.74 per share, resulting in a forward yield of 3.35%. The company, one of the largest utilities in the U.S., shows steady revenue growth and strong operating margins but faces liquidity concerns and a high debt-to-equity ratio. Investors should consider the company's financial leverage and regulatory risks, particularly in Georgia, despite its stable dividend policy.

feed.title: Packaging Corp of America (PKG) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America (PKG) reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results, with increased net sales and EBITDA, despite facing challenges such as decreased EPS in Q4 due to lower production and sales volume, higher operating costs, and inventory management issues from recent acquisitions. The company is actively integrating acquired assets and expects operational improvements and cost structure benefits in the coming months, with the CEO confirming expectations to run the mill system at full capacity throughout 2026.

feed.title: Motorola Solutions earnings: Q4 and 2025 results coming Feb. 11
feed.summary: Motorola Solutions, Inc. (NYSE: MSI) announced it will release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 earnings results on Wednesday, February 11, after market close. The company will also host a conference call for financial analysts at 4 p.m. Central (5 p.m. Eastern) on the same day, with a live webcast available on their investor relations website. This announcement includes contact information for investor and media inquiries.

feed.title: Huntington Bank Construction Stratford Road
feed.summary: Construction has begun on a new Huntington Bank branch located at 594 South Stratford Road in Winston-Salem, N.C. The newspaper reported on the development on January 28, 2026. This adds another branch for Huntington Bank in the area.

feed.title: Inside Southwest Airlines’ central nervous system for its 4000+ daily flights
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines has unveiled a new, sleek network operations control (NOC) floor, a 37,000 square foot hardened facility designed to withstand a severe tornado. This nerve center, where dozens of employees monitor and coordinate the airline's over 4,000 daily flights, is a significant investment made after a 2022 meltdown cost the airline over $1.1 billion. The new facility, equipped with multiple redundancy systems and a focus on employee comfort, aims to enhance Southwest's reliability and on-time performance, which has notably improved, leading to a waived $11 million fine from the U.S. Department of Transportation.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Reports 2025 Fourth Quarter Results
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson (Nasdaq: CHRW) reported its financial results for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, highlighting continued strong performance despite market headwinds, driven by market share gains and its Lean AI strategy. The company achieved a 1.7% increase in adjusted diluted EPS to $1.23 for the fourth quarter and a 12.9% increase for the full year 2025 to $5.09. Key operational successes included volume growth in North American Surface Transportation and expanded gross margins in Global Forwarding, alongside significant cash generation and returns to shareholders.

feed.title: Lam Research Corporation Reports Financial Results for the Quarter Ended December 28, 2025
feed.summary: Lam Research Corporation announced its financial results for the quarter ended December 28, 2025, reporting revenues of $5.34 billion. The company achieved U.S. GAAP diluted EPS of $1.26 and non-GAAP diluted EPS of $1.27. Lam Research also provided guidance for the quarter ending March 29, 2026, anticipating revenues of approximately $5.70 billion.

feed.title: SL Green Realty Corp. Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 EPS of ($1.49) Per Share; and FFO of $1.13 Per Share
feed.summary: SL Green Realty Corp. (NYSE: SLG) reported a net loss attributable to common stockholders of $1.49 per share for the fourth quarter of 2025 and $1.61 per share for the full year 2025. The company's Funds from Operations (FFO) were $1.13 per share for Q4 2025 and $5.72 per share for the full year. Key operational highlights include signing 766,783 square feet of Manhattan office leases in Q4 2025, with occupancy increasing to 93.0% by year-end, and significant investment and financing activities.

feed.title: AMGEN ANNOUNCES WEBCAST OF 2025 FOURTH QUARTER AND FULL YEAR FINANCIAL RESULTS
feed.summary: Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) has announced that it will report its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results on Tuesday, February 3, 2026, after the U.S. market closes. A conference call with management, led by chairman and CEO Robert A. Bradway, will follow at 4:30 p.m. ET, and a live audio webcast will be accessible on Amgen's Investor Relations site, with a replay available for at least 90 days. Historically, similar webcast announcements have resulted in modest stock price movements, while actual earnings releases have sometimes shown divergence from strong reported metrics due to high investor expectations.

feed.title: Tesla Posts Fourth-Quarter Profit That Beats Expectations
feed.summary: Tesla Inc. announced plans to invest $2 billion into Elon Musk's AI company, xAI, which they believe will enhance Tesla's ability to develop and deploy AI products and services. This announcement came alongside their report of higher-than-expected profit for the fourth quarter. The company stated this investment aims to expand its AI capabilities into the physical world at scale.

feed.title: Who bought TikTok? What to know about a Florida billionaire, Oracle
feed.summary: TikTok has officially changed hands, with a U.S.-backed group of investors and companies, including Oracle, taking a majority 80.1% stake, while ByteDance retains 19.9%. The deal follows years of scrutiny over national security risks and President Trump's previous attempts to ban the app. The new ownership has led to updated terms of service, with some users and celebrities, like Megan Stalter, claiming content censorship, particularly regarding criticism of the Trump administration.

feed.title: Danaher stock price target raised to $254 from $250 at Evercore ISI
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has increased its price target for Danaher (NYSE:DHR) to $254 from $250, while maintaining an Outperform rating. The adjustment follows Danaher's fourth-quarter results, which met expectations, and despite the company trading above InvestingPro's Fair Value estimate, Evercore sees potential for upside, particularly in BPS Equipment and Life Sciences. The new price target reflects approximately 30 times the calendar year 2026 price-to-earnings ratio and a 3% free cash flow yield.

feed.title: Textron Aviation reports 36% revenue increase as manufacturer recovers from strike
feed.summary: Textron Aviation reported a significant increase in revenue, reaching $1.7 billion in Q4, indicating a recovery in production following a machinists' strike the previous year. The article also mentions the financial results of Textron Inc.'s subsidiary Bell, which is involved in developing the MV-75 helicopter for the Army with manufacturing operations in Wichita.

feed.title: Clorox Fined $14.15M For Delayed Warning About Pine-Sol Bacteria
feed.summary: Clorox has been fined $14.15 million by the Consumer Product Safety Commission for failing to promptly report bacterial contamination in certain Pine-Sol products. The company reportedly identified potential contamination years before a recall was announced in October 2022, leading to the penalty. The settlement also requires Clorox to strengthen its internal controls and submit compliance reports.

feed.title: |‘Clean, efficient, reliable’: $3 million TVA donation transforms UT’s nuclear engineering department
feed.summary: |The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) has donated $3 million to the University of Tennessee's nuclear engineering department, creating an endowment for a departmental chair. This investment aims to support the growing enrollment in the program, which has tripled in the past three years, by hiring more teaching faculty and reducing the burden on existing staff. The donation highlights the increasing demand for nuclear energy as a sustainable power source and positions UT and Tennessee at the forefront of the industry's expansion.

feed.title: Research Alert: CFRA Cuts Opinion On Shares Of Dollar Tree, Inc. To Sell From Hold
feed.summary: CFRA has downgraded its opinion on Dollar Tree, Inc. (DLTR) shares from "Hold" to "Sell" and reduced its 12-month target price to $110 from $126. This downgrade is attributed to slowing comparable sales growth, negative traffic trends, and potential competitive responses to the multi-price initiative. Additionally, risks from decelerating average ticket growth and potential SNAP changes in 2026 are highlighted.

feed.title: Entrata works with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan on planned IPO - Bloomberg
feed.summary: Property software company Entrata is reportedly working with Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase to prepare for an initial public offering. The company, backed by Silver Lake and Blackstone Inc., aims to complete the share sale as soon as April, potentially raising around $500 million. Details including the size and valuation are still in development, with additional banks possibly joining the underwriting team.

feed.title: Dollar Tree faces new threat as affordable budget store rival eyes top spot
feed.summary: Dollar Tree is facing increasing competition from rival discount stores like Dollar General's Popshelf and Five Below. Popshelf, launched in 2020, offers a wider range of products, including home decor and cleaning supplies, with prices averaging around $5 but still featuring some $1 items, which has attracted customers due to better store design and organization. Five Below is also thriving by diversifying its merchandise to appeal to tweens and teens and moving beyond its strict $5 or under policy.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson MedTech Marks $100M in Medtech Product Donations
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson MedTech has announced it has donated $100 million in medtech products since 2020 to improve global access to care. The company also plans to commit an additional $20 million in 2026 for programs and investments supporting health workers in providing cardiac, vision, and surgical care. These initiatives are part of the J&J CareCommunity social impact platform, aiming to close care gaps through partnerships with organizations like Operation Smile and the American Heart Association.

feed.title: Applied Materials Upgrades Link AI Capex Boom To Stretch Valuation Concerns
feed.summary: Multiple major brokerages have upgraded Applied Materials (NasdaqGS:AMAT) due to an anticipated increase in semiconductor capital spending driven by AI. Applied Materials, a key supplier of wafer fabrication equipment, is expected to benefit from increased capital expenditures by chipmakers like TSMC and Intel. While broker upgrades link the company directly to AI-related growth, the stock is currently flagged as trading significantly above its estimated fair value by Simply Wall St.

feed.title: Twin Peaks parent company files for bankruptcy. Will Oklahoma locations be affected?
feed.summary: The parent company of Twin Peaks, Great American Cookie, Marble Slab Creamery, and Fazoli's, FAT Brands, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy due to debt and aggressive acquisitions. Despite the filing, Oklahoma's locations for these brands, including Twin Peaks, Fazoli's, Marble Slab Creamery, and Great American Cookie, are expected to remain open and operational during the proceedings. The company aims to use the bankruptcy process to strengthen its balance sheet and maintain service to customers and support for franchise partners.

feed.title: Exploring Selective Insurance Gr's Earnings Expectations
feed.summary: Selective Insurance Gr (NASDAQ: SIGI) is set to release its quarterly earnings report on January 29, 2026, with analysts anticipating an EPS of $2.21. The company's recent financial performance shows a positive revenue trend but lags behind industry peers in revenue growth, net margin, return on equity, and return on assets, while also carrying a higher debt-to-equity ratio. Despite a "Neutral" consensus analyst rating and a modest 1.4% potential upside based on an average price target of $81.75, long-term shareholders are likely bearish given the stock's 1.29% decline over the past 52 weeks.

feed.title: Syngenta, Chevron Settle Paraquat Case Before 1st Philly Trial
feed.summary: Syngenta and Chevron have settled the first paraquat Parkinson's disease mass tort case that was scheduled to go to trial in Philadelphia. The settlement occurred on the eve of the trial, resolving the dispute between the involved parties. Law360 reported on this development, highlighting its significance in the context of paraquat litigation.

feed.title: User | thepilotnews.com - PGIM Introduces Two Active Muni ETFs
feed.summary: PGIM has introduced two new actively managed municipal bond ETFs. These ETFs aim to provide investors with opportunities in the municipal bond market, leveraging active management strategies. The launch reflects PGIM's expansion into the exchange-traded fund space for municipal securities.

feed.title: Broadridge Financial Solutions stock hits 52-week low at $204.55
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) stock recently hit a 52-week low of $204.55, reflecting a significant decline in its market performance over the past year. Despite the downturn and negative short-term returns, an InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued with potential for growth. The company also announced a strong fiscal Q1 2026 and an acquisition, while analysts have adjusted price targets.

feed.title: Edison International (NYSE:EIX) Price Target Raised to $66.00
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has raised its price target for Edison International (EIX) to $66.00 from $65.00, reiterating a "neutral" rating. This implies a potential upside of 6.14% from the previous close, with the company's stock currently trading around $62.18. The consensus among analysts remains a "Hold" with a target price of $63.30, considering mixed opinions from various brokerages.

feed.title: Jefferies Financial Group Cuts Edison International (NYSE:EIX) Price Target to $54.00
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has lowered its price target for Edison International (NYSE:EIX) from $59.00 to $54.00, while maintaining a "hold" rating, suggesting a potential downside of 13.16%. This adjustment comes amidst mixed analyst sentiment, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average target price of $63.30. Edison International recently reported stronger-than-expected quarterly earnings and revenue, but the stock currently trades at $62.18 with a market cap of approximately $23.9 billion.

feed.title: DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) Price Target Raised to $153.00 at Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has raised its price target for DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) to $153.00 from $150.00, maintaining a "buy" rating and implying a 12.20% upside. This adjustment comes amidst a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $147.62, following DTE's recent earnings beat and positive FY2025 and FY2026 EPS guidance. The company's stock currently trades around $136.37 with a P/E of approximately 20.5.

feed.title: Notable Wednesday Option Activity: IBM, TBCH, OKLO
feed.summary: This article highlights noteworthy options trading activity for International Business Machines Corp (IBM), Turtle Beach Corp (TBCH), and Oklo Inc (OKLO) on Wednesday. It details the total contracts traded for each company, the percentage of their average daily trading volume, and specifically mentions high-volume strike prices and expiration dates for certain options. The information suggests significant investor interest in these particular options.

feed.title: Western Digital Stock Rises On Seagate's Strong Q2 Results
feed.summary: Western Digital (WDC) shares are trading higher after peer Seagate Technology Holdings (STX) reported strong Q2 results, surpassing analyst expectations for earnings and revenue. Seagate's success, driven by strong cloud and data-center demand fueled by AI, suggests positive industry tailwinds that could benefit Western Digital when it reports its own Q2 earnings on Thursday. Western Digital's stock was up over 12% following Seagate's upbeat report.

feed.title: Southern Company declares quarterly dividend of 74 cents per share
feed.summary: Southern Company (NYSE:SO) has announced a regular quarterly dividend of 74 cents per share, payable on March 6, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 17, 2026. The company has a strong dividend history, maintaining or increasing payments for 78 consecutive years. This announcement comes amid recent analyst downgrades and updates, and operational developments regarding power generation capacity expansion.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Roper Technologies (NASDAQ:ROP) Stock Price
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has downgraded Roper Technologies (NASDAQ:ROP) with a pessimistic forecast, cutting its price target from $457 to $397 and assigning an "underweight" rating. This follows Roper beating Q4 adjusted EPS but missing revenue expectations and providing Q1 and FY2026 guidance that was below Street estimates, leading to a stock selloff towards its 52-week low. Several other analysts have also trimmed their price targets for the industrial products company.

feed.title: Bank of Montreal (TSE:BMO) Stock Rating Upgraded by TD Securities
feed.summary: TD Securities has upgraded Bank of Montreal (TSE:BMO) to a "Buy" rating from "Hold," increasing its price target to C$209, suggesting an 11.08% upside. Despite this upgrade, the MarketBeat consensus remains a "Hold" with an average target of C$185.17. The article also details recent analyst reports and the bank's financial performance, including its Q4 EPS of C$3.28 and current trading price of C$188.15.

feed.title: Camden Reportedly Looking To Exit California With $1.5B Portfolio Sale
feed.summary: Camden Property Trust is reportedly looking to sell its entire portfolio of 11 multifamily properties in California for over $1.5 billion. This potential divestment, driven by frustrations with the state's regulatory environment, is being marketed through JLL and would involve approximately 3,600 units. Other multifamily operators have also considered or initiated exits from California due to similar challenges.
